description: A mysterious illustrated codex written in an undeciphered script, dated to the early 15th century.
An ancient Greek hand-powered orrery, sometimes called the first analog computer.
A large explosion that occurred near the Tunguska River in Siberia.
A strong narrowband radio signal detected by Ohio State's Big Ear radio telescope in 1977.
A railroad worker who survived an iron rod being driven through his skull in 1848.
The observation that hot water can, under certain conditions, freeze faster than cold water.
An English colony established in 1587 on Roanoke Island whose inhabitants disappeared.
Microscopic animals known for their extraordinary resilience to extreme environments.
An ultra-low-frequency underwater sound detected by NOAA in 1997.
Studies showing sham surgeries can produce real therapeutic improvements.
An unexplained atmospheric phenomenon involving luminous spherical objects.
A cognitive bias in which people with limited knowledge overestimate their competence.
